author: śrīla locana⋅dāsa ṭhākura
verses:
bhaja bhaja hari, mana dṛḍha kari’,
mukhe bola tā̐’ra nāma
vrajendra-nandana, gopī⋅prāṇadhana,
bhuvana-mohana śyāma

kakhana maribe, kemane taribe,
viṣama śamana ḍāke
jā̐hāra pratāpe, bhuvana kā̐paye,
nā jāni mara vipāke

kula⋅dhana pāiyā, unamata haiyā,
āpanāke jāna baḍa
śamanera dūte, dhari’ pāye hāte,
bā̐dhiyā karibe jaḍa

kibā jati, satī, kibā nīca jāti,
jei hari nāhi bhaje
tabe janamiyā, bhramiyā bhramiyā,
raurava⋅narake maje

e dāsa locana, bhāve anukṣaṇa,
vṛthāi janama gela
hari nā bhajinu, viṣaye majinu,
hṛdaye rahala śela
en_translation:
Worship Hari, making your mind firm. Speak His name with your mouth—the son of the King of Vraja, the treasure of the gopīs' lives, the dark-complexioned Lord who enchants the universe.

When will you die? How will you be saved? The fierce lord of death calls. By whose prowess the universe trembles, you do not know when you will die in great distress.

Having obtained family and wealth and becoming intoxicated by them, you consider yourself great. The messengers of the lord of death, catching you by your hands and feet, will bind you and make you helpless.

Whether an ascetic, a chaste woman, or one of a low caste, whoever does not worship the Lord will wander through countless lives and drown in a hellish planet.

This servant Locana constantly thinks: "My life has passed in vain! I did not worship the Lord and instead drowned in worldly affairs; a spear of regret remains in my heart."
